markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Expose project-level graph views with performance limits and hub suppression. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: GlobalGraphApi leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: GlobalGraphApi is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Project-level global graph API with performance limits + hub suppression over loom_edges + loom_blocks (+ MT-177 bridge for citations). Read projection; no parallel store.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - global_graph (per-node degree across filtered edges; hub suppression drops degree>threshold nodes + lists them; node_limit -> truncated) reusing assemble_loom_graph

evidence.result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed (real PostgreSQL) [shared MT-179+180 suite]
tests: global_graph_returns_all_and_suppresses_hubs - threshold 0 disables suppression (hub present, degree 6 counted); threshold 5 suppresses the degree-6 hub (dropped + in suppressed_hub_ids); unrelated X-Y pair unaffected; no edges to a suppressed hub
global_graph_node_limit_sets_truncated - node_limit 3 over 5 blocks -> 3 nodes + truncated=true

evidence: Status flip note (2026-06-12T06:25:27.785Z): Loom engine group backend-complete per delegate report - real-PG proven (isolated schemas, full migration chain), per-MT adversarial review recorded in this file. SQLite removed/unreachable (orphans git-rm'd, sqlx has no sqlite feature, source regression guard added). Markdown-vault-never-authority proven via projection-delete byte-identity negative test. Stable /workspaces/:ws/loom/* HTTP contracts documented for the Notes UI. Implementation commit(s): cd944110.

findings: HUB-SUPPRESSION GUARD (handled+tested): hub_degree_threshold>0 required, so threshold 0 disables suppression deterministically.
INDUCED SUBGRAPH (handled+tested): edges require BOTH endpoints in the surviving node set, so a suppressed hub's edges are excluded.
DOS (mitigated): node_limit.clamp(1,5000) bounds the expensive block/edge fetches; degree is one aggregate query.
ALL-IDS FETCH (accepted): the candidate id list is the full workspace block id set (cheap id-only fetch) filtered+capped in Rust; the heavy block/edge fetches use only the capped node set.
EDGE-TYPE INJECTION (clear): LoomEdgeType enum parse; ids bound as TEXT[].
DETERMINISM (clear): blocks ordered updated_at DESC, block_id ASC; suppressed_hub_ids sorted.
fixes: No code change required; findings are handled-by-design (and tested) or accepted with rationale.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-180 requires a project-level global Loom graph API with performance node limits and hub suppression as a PostgreSQL projection over loom_edges + loom_blocks. The code delivers exactly that: a real storage trait method, PostgreSQL implementation, wired HTTP route, and two real-PostgreSQL integration tests proving hub suppression and node-limit truncation. No placeholders, no SQLite/mock fallback, aligned with current Master Spec [LM-NOTES-002].
findings: storage/postgres.rs:9797-9884 global_graph: real PostgreSQL aggregation — per-node degree via UNION ALL of source/target over filtered loom_edges (9814-9832), hub suppression drops degree>threshold nodes (threshold 0 disables) and lists them in suppressed_hub_ids (9840-9846), candidate ids capped via node_limit.clamp(1,5000) with truncated set when full set exceeds cap (9868-9869).

storage/postgres.rs:71-246 assemble_loom_graph builds induced subgraph (edges require BOTH endpoints in surviving node set, 9 lines of real SQL at ~187-204), so a suppressed hub's edges are excluded — matches the test assertion.

api/loom.rs:269-272 route /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/graph/global registered; handler 3284-3305 parses edge_types, applies LOOM_GLOBAL_GRAPH_DEFAULT_NODE_LIMIT(500)/MAX(5000)/DEFAULT_HUB_DEGREE(50) consts (loom.rs:1009-1013) and calls storage.global_graph.

storage/mod.rs:2581-2584 declares Database::global_graph at the trait boundary with doc describing truncated + suppressed_hub_ids; LoomGraph struct (loom.rs:776-778,996) carries truncated + suppressed_hub_ids fields.

tests/loom_graph_tests.rs:181-237 two global-graph tests run against real PostgreSQL: global_graph_returns_all_and_suppresses_hubs proves threshold 0 keeps hub (degree 6 counted), threshold 5 drops the degree-6 hub + lists it + excludes its edges + leaves unrelated X-Y pair intact; global_graph_node_limit_sets_truncated proves node_limit 3 over 5 blocks -> 3 nodes + truncated=true.

knowledge_pg_support.rs:21,40 confirms NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback — tests resolve POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL against isolated migrated schemas and skip (not mock) if PG unavailable; sub_rule_2 real-resource touch satisfied.
Placeholder scan of global/graph/assemble/hub/suppress paths in postgres.rs returned zero hits (no todo!/unimplemented!/Unavailable/stub).

No forbidden deps: pure PostgreSQL projection, no markdown vault, no external search/graph engine, no SQLite. Aside: lifecycle paperwork (claimed_by/completed_by null, self-review sub-agent) is non-blocking per ground rules.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
